academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need an upper secondary school diploma (or equivalent) with at least 12 years of schooling suitable for access to higher education. For the foreign-qualification calls, the entry qualification must be obtained by 31 July 2026. If the school system is shorter than 12 years, applicants must add the required extra academic study, post-secondary qualification, or an Italian foundation course.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: No programme-specific school-subject prerequisite was stated beyond meeting the CEnT-S admission-test rules.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: No GPA requirement was stated. Applicants must verify the country-based entry-title rules, and if their home system requires a university entrance exam for higher education access, proof of passing that exam is required.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 (CEFR) or equivalent, to be achieved by 31 July 2026.
accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum 6.0 for B2.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum 126 for B2.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum 65 for B2.
exemption_rules: No certificate is required if the previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
No certificate is required for native English speakers from the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The second EU and EU-equated round was officially confirmed on the admissions page after the first-round ranking list.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Single non-EU call for applicants residing outside Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: EU-equivalent applicants may use a valid ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants already residing in Italy.
Documents must be official and written or translated in Italian or English.

required_documents.document_name: Upper secondary school certificate or academic qualification (if already obtained)
required_documents.notes: No legalised documents are required at application stage.
Listed in the general application document checklist.
Listed in the general application document checklist.
Required unless the applicant qualifies for an exemption.

required_documents.document_name: CEnT-S provisional result and final normalized score upload
required_documents.notes: The score must be uploaded to apply.unipd.it after the test and by the relevant macro-period deadline.

required_documents.document_name: Legalised upper secondary diploma translated into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Proof of passed home-country university entrance exam, if required by the education system
required_documents.notes: Required only when the country of qualification requires such an exam for university access.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and D visa for study-enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol on Universitaly by 15 July and then apply for a study visa.

entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants. The main non-EU and first EU or EU-equated calls use tests taken by the end of the second macro-period; the confirmed second EU or EU-equated round accepts tests taken by the end of the third macro-period.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: First macro-period: 27 November 2025, 16 December 2025, 15 January 2026 (resit 20 January 2026). Second macro-period: 19 February 2026, 26 February 2026, 12 March 2026 (resit 19 March 2026). For the second EU or EU-equated round: 7 May 2026, 21 May 2026, 9 June 2026, 16 June 2026 (resit 18 June 2026).
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Ranking uses weighted CEnT-S sections Mathematics, Reasoning on texts and data, Physics, and Chemistry (weight 1 each; Biology weight 0). In the non-EU call, inclusion in the ranking requires a normalized weighted score of at least 38 and at least 7 correct answers in Mathematics. The official second-round English instructions state that the same procedures as the first EU call apply.

uncertainty_notes: The official 2026/2027 information is split across the programme page, the apply.unipd.it factsheet, and multiple admission notices; this JSON consolidates them into one record.
